pattern: Backtracking from each position tries every dictionary word that matches the next slice, recording full segmentations as space-joined sentences.
hints: Build sentences by repeatedly choosing a dictionary word that the remaining suffix starts with.
From a position, branch on every word that matches there, recurse on what remains, and prepend the chosen word to each sentence the suffix produces.
Memoize on the start index returning the list of completions for that suffix; join the chosen word and each completion with a single space, and the suffix at the end yields one empty completion.

edge_inputs.description: no valid segmentation yields empty list

stress.description: short all-a string with overlapping single and double words, many sentences
stress.note: kept short because the number of segmentations grows fast
